                [32m'No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>'[39m
            },
            {
              type: [32m'text'[39m,
              text: [32m'# RedwoodSDK: HTTP Session Store backed by Cloudflare KV\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Background\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'You are extending a freshly scaffolded RedwoodSDK (`rwsdk`) project. Implement an HTTP session store backed by a Cloudflare KV binding (named `SESSIONS`) configured in `wrangler.jsonc`. Locally, `npm run dev` runs the app under Vite + workerd (miniflare), which provides an in-process KV implementation, so **no Cloudflare account, account ID, or API token is required** for this task.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m"The API must expose endpoints to create, read, delete, and count sessions. Session data lives in KV under the key prefix `sess:` and is automatically expired by KV's TTL feature.\n"[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Requirements\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The web app is a RedwoodSDK project served by `npm run dev` (Vite + Cloudflare workerd / miniflare).\n'[39m +
                [32m"* Configure a Cloudflare KV binding named `SESSIONS` in `wrangler.jsonc` so that it is available as `env.SESSIONS` (or via rwsdk's worker `env`) at runtime.\n"[39m +
                [32m'* Implement the following JSON HTTP endpoints (all under `/api/sessions`):\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `POST /api/sessions` — creates a session.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `GET /api/sessions/me` — reads the current session via the `sid` cookie.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `DELETE /api/sessions/me` — deletes the current session.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `GET /api/sessions/count` — returns the number of sessions currently stored in KV.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Session records must be stored in KV under keys of the form `sess:<sessionId>`. The value MUST be JSON with shape `{userId, createdAt, expiresAt}`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Session ids must be random 32-character lowercase hex strings (i.e. 16 bytes of randomness, hex-encoded).\n'[39m +
                [32m"* Sessions must expire after 3600 seconds. Use KV's `expirationTtl` (3600) on write so KV evicts expired entries automatically.\n"[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Implementation Hints\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Start from the existing scaffold at the project path below; the `rwsdk` dependency, Vite config, and `wrangler.jsonc` skeleton are already in place. You need to add the `SESSIONS` KV namespace binding to `wrangler.jsonc` (the local dev runtime does not require a real `id` — a placeholder string is sufficient for miniflare).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Read https://docs.rwsdk.com/llms-full.txt and the Cloudflare KV docs (https://developers.cloudflare.com/kv/api/) for the binding shape and the `get`, `put`, `delete`, and `list` methods.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Inside a RedwoodSDK route handler, the Cloudflare bindings are available via the worker `env` (e.g. `env.SESSIONS.put(...)`). You can use `route("/api/sessions", { post: handler })` or define a handler that switches on `request.method`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Generate session ids with the Web Crypto API: `crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(16))` then hex-encode.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* For cookie parsing, read the `Cookie` request header and parse out the `sid` value yourself — no extra library is required.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Use `SESSIONS.list({ prefix: "sess:" })` to enumerate live session keys for the count endpoint. Remember that `list()` may paginate via `cursor`/`list_complete`; iterate until `list_complete` is true so the count is accurate when there are many keys.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Set the response `Content-Type` header to `application/json` for all JSON responses.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Acceptance Criteria\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Project path: /home/user/myproject\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Start command: npm run dev\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Port: 5173\n'[39m +
                [32m'* KV binding: A binding named `SESSIONS` is configured in `wrangler.jsonc` and is reachable from route handlers at runtime.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* KV key naming: All session entries are stored under keys matching the pattern `sess:<sessionId>`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Session id format: 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters (`^[0-9a-f]{32}$`).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Session TTL: 3600 seconds, enforced via KV `expirationTtl` on write.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* API endpoints:\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `POST /api/sessions`\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Request: `Content-Type: application/json`, body `{"userId": string}`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response status: `201`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response `Content-Type: application/json`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response body shape:\n'[39m +
                [32m'      ```json\n'[39m +
                [32m'      {\n'[39m +
                [32m'        "sessionId": string,\n'[39m +
                [32m'        "expiresAt": number\n'[39m +
                [32m'      }\n'[39m +
                [32m'      ```\n'[39m +
                [32m'      `sessionId` MUST match `^[0-9a-f]{32}$`. `expiresAt` is a Unix timestamp in **seconds** approximately equal to `now + 3600` (within a 60-second tolerance).\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response headers MUST include a `Set-Cookie` header that:\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Sets the cookie named `sid` to the same value as `sessionId`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Includes the `HttpOnly` attribute (case-insensitive).\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Includes a `Path=/` attribute.\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Includes `Max-Age=3600` (case-insensitive).\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `GET /api/sessions/me`\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * When the request carries a valid `sid` cookie whose corresponding KV entry exists:\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Response status: `200`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Response `Content-Type: application/json`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Response body shape:\n'[39m +
                [32m'        ```json\n'[39m +
                [32m'        {\n'[39m +
                [32m'          "userId": string,\n'[39m +
                [32m'          "createdAt": number,\n'[39m +
                [32m'          "expiresAt": number\n'[39m +
                [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                [32m'        ```\n'[39m +
                [32m'        The `userId` MUST equal the one supplied when the session was created.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * When the `sid` cookie is missing, malformed, or its KV entry does not exist:\n'[39m +
                [32m'      * Response status: `401`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `DELETE /api/sessions/me`\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * On success (request carries a valid `sid` cookie): response status `204`, no body, and a `Set-Cookie` header that clears the `sid` cookie (`sid=` with `Max-Age=0`, plus `HttpOnly` and `Path=/`).\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * After this call, the corresponding `sess:<sessionId>` key MUST be removed from KV.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `GET /api/sessions/count`\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response status: `200`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response `Content-Type: application/json`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'    * Response body shape:\n'[39m +
                [32m'      ```json\n'[39m +
                [32m'      {\n'[39m +
                [32m'        "count": number\n'[39m +
                [32m'      }\n'[39m +
                [32m'      ```\n'[39m +
                [32m'      where `count` is the total number of live keys in KV with the `sess:` prefix (use `SESSIONS.list({ prefix: "sess:" })`, iterating through all pages).'[39m
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          role: [32m'assistant'[39m,

          content: [
            {
              type: [32m'tool-result'[39m,
              toolCallId: [32m'IsAB5wuWZQLPcdty'[39m,
              toolName: [32m'executeCommand'[39m,
              output: {
                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  output: [32m'import React from "react";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { RequestInfo } from "../requestInfo/types";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import type { DocumentProps, LayoutProps } from "./types.js";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type MaybePromise<T> = T | Promise<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type BivariantRouteHandler<T extends RequestInfo, R> = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    bivarianceHack(requestInfo: T): R;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}["bivarianceHack"];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type RouteMiddleware<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = BivariantRouteHandler<T, MaybePromise<React.JSX.Element | Response | void>>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type ExceptHandler<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    __rwExcept: true;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    handler: (error: unknown, requestInfo: T) => MaybePromise<React.JSX.Element | Response | void>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    pathPattern?: string;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type RouteFunction<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = BivariantRouteHandler<T, MaybePromise<Response>>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type RouteComponent<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = BivariantRouteHandler<T, MaybePromise<React.JSX.Element | Response | void>>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type RouteHandler<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = RouteFunction<T> | RouteComponent<T> | readonly [...RouteMiddleware<T>[], RouteFunction<T> | RouteComponent<T>];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'declare const METHOD_VERBS: readonly ["delete", "get", "head", "patch", "post", "put"];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type MethodVerb = (typeof METHOD_VERBS)[number];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type MethodHandlers<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    [K in MethodVerb]?: RouteHandler<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'} & {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    config?: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        disable405?: true;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        disableOptions?: true;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    custom?: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        [method: string]: RouteHandler<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type Route<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = RouteMiddleware<T> | RouteDefinition<string, T> | ExceptHandler<T> | readonly Route<T>[];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type NormalizedRouteDefinition<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    path: string;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    handler: RouteHandler<T> | MethodHandlers<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    layouts?: React.FC<LayoutProps<T>>[];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type RouteDefinition<Path extends string = string, T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo> = NormalizedRouteDefinition<T> & {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    readonly __rwPath?: Path;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type TrimTrailingSlash<S extends string> = S extends `${infer Head}/` ? TrimTrailingSlash<Head> : S;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type TrimLeadingSlash<S extends string> = S extends `/${infer Rest}` ? TrimLeadingSlash<Rest> : S;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type NormalizePrefix<Prefix extends string> = TrimTrailingSlash<TrimLeadingSlash<Prefix>> extends "" ? "" : `/${TrimTrailingSlash<TrimLeadingSlash<Prefix>>}`;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type NormalizePath<Path extends string> = TrimTrailingSlash<Path> extends "/" ? "/" : `/${TrimTrailingSlash<TrimLeadingSlash<Path>>}`;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type JoinPaths<Prefix extends string, Path extends string> = NormalizePrefix<Prefix> extends "" ? NormalizePath<Path> : Path extends "/" ? NormalizePrefix<Prefix> : `${NormalizePrefix<Prefix>}${NormalizePath<Path>}`;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type PrefixedRouteValue<Prefix extends string, Value> = Value extends RouteDefinition<infer Path, infer Req> ? RouteDefinition<JoinPaths<Prefix, Path>, Req> : Value extends ExceptHandler<any> ? Value : Value extends readonly Route<any>[] ? PrefixedRouteArray<Prefix, Value> : Value;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type PrefixedRouteArray<Prefix extends string, Routes extends readonly Route<any>[]> = Routes extends readonly [] ? [] : Routes extends readonly [infer Head, ...infer Tail] ? readonly [\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    PrefixedRouteValue<Prefix, Head>,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ...PrefixedRouteArray<Prefix, Tail extends readonly Route<any>[] ? Tail : []>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'] : ReadonlyArray<PrefixedRouteValue<Prefix, Routes[number]>>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function matchPath<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(routePath: string, requestPath: string): T["params"] | null;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function defineRoutes<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(routes: readonly Route<T>[]): {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    routes: Route<T>[];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    handle: ({ request, renderPage, getRequestInfo, onError, runWithRequestInfoOverrides, rscActionHandler, }: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        request: Request;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        renderPage: (requestInfo: T, Page: React.FC, onError: (error: unknown) => void) => Promise<Response>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        getRequestInfo: () => T;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        onError: (error: unknown) => void;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        runWithRequestInfoOverrides: <Result>(overrides: Partial<T>, fn: () => Promise<Result>) => Promise<Result>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        rscActionHandler: (request: Request) => Promise<unknown>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }) => Response | Promise<Response>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Defines a route handler for a path pattern.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Supports three types of path patterns:\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - Static: /about, /contact\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - Parameters: /users/:id, /posts/:postId/edit\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - Wildcards: /files/\\*, /api/\\*\\/download\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Static route\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * route("/about", () => <AboutPage />)\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Route with parameters\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * route("/users/:id", ({ params }) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return <UserProfile userId={params.id} />\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Route with wildcards\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * route("/files/*", ({ params }) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   const filePath = params.$0\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return <FileViewer path={filePath} />\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Method-based routing\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * route("/api/users", {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   get: () => Response.json(users),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   post: ({ request }) => Response.json({ status: "created" }, { status: 201 }),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   delete: () => new Response(null, { status: 204 }),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Route with middleware array\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * route("/admin", [isAuthenticated, isAdmin, () => <AdminDashboard />])\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function route<Path extends string, T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(path: Path, handler: RouteHandler<T> | MethodHandlers<T>): RouteDefinition<NormalizePath<Path>, T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Defines a route handler for the root path "/".\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Homepage\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * index(() => <HomePage />)\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // With middleware\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * index([logRequest, () => <HomePage />])\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function index<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(handler: RouteHandler<T>): RouteDefinition<"/", T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Defines an error handler that catches errors from routes, middleware, and RSC actions.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Global error handler\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * except((error, requestInfo) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   console.error(error);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Error handler that returns a React component\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * except((error) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return <ErrorPage error={error} />;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function except<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(handler: (error: unknown, requestInfo: T) => MaybePromise<React.JSX.Element | Response | void>): ExceptHandler<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function prefix<Prefix extends string, T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route<T>[] = readonly Route<T>[]>(prefixPath: Prefix, routes: Routes): PrefixedRouteArray<Prefix, Routes>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare const wrapHandlerToThrowResponses: <T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo>(handler: RouteFunction<T> | RouteComponent<T>) => RouteHandler<T>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Wraps routes with a layout component.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Define a layout component\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * function BlogLayout({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return (\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     <div>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *       <nav>Blog Navigation</nav>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *       <main>{children}</main>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     </div>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   )\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Apply layout to routes\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * const blogRoutes = layout(BlogLayout, [\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   route("/", () => <BlogIndex />),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   route("/post/:id", ({ params }) => <BlogPost id={params.id} />),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ])\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function layout<TLayout extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route<any>[] = readonly Route<any>[]>(LayoutComponent: React.FC<LayoutProps<TLayout>>, routes: Routes): Routes;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Wraps routes with a Document component and configures rendering options.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m" * @param options.rscPayload - Toggle the RSC payload that's appended to the Document. Disabling this will mean that interactivity no longer works.\r\n"[39m +
                    [32m' * @param options.ssr - Disable sever side rendering for all these routes. This only allow client side rendering, which requires `rscPayload` to be enabled.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // Basic usage\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * defineApp([\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   render(Document, [\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     route("/", () => <HomePage />),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     route("/about", () => <AboutPage />),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   ]),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ])\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // With custom rendering options\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * render(Document, [\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   route("/", () => <HomePage />),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ], {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   rscPayload: true,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   ssr: true,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type RenderedRoutes<T extends RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route<T>[]> = readonly [RouteMiddleware<T>, ...Routes];\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function render<T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route<T>[] = readonly Route<T>[]>(Document: React.FC<DocumentProps<T>>, routes: Routes, options?: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    rscPayload?: boolean;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ssr?: boolean;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}): RenderedRoutes<T, Routes>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare const isClientReference: (value: any) => boolean;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export {};\r\n'[39m +

                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  output: [32m'type Interruptor<TArgs extends any[] = any[]> = (context: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    request: Request;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ctx: Record<string, any>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    args: TArgs;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}) => Promise<Response | void> | Response | void;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type ServerFunction<TArgs extends any[] = any[], TResult = any> = (...args: TArgs) => Promise<TResult>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type ServerFunctionOptions = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    method?: "GET" | "POST";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'type WrappedServerFunction<TArgs extends any[] = any[], TResult = any> = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    (...args: TArgs): Promise<TResult>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    method?: "GET" | "POST";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type ServerFunctionWrap = (fn: Function, args: any[], type: "action" | "query") => Promise<any>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Register a wrapper that runs around every server action and query handler.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Call this once in your worker entry point. The wrapper receives the main\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * handler function, its arguments, and the type ("action" or "query").\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Interruptors run *outside* the wrapper.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Only one wrapper is active at a time; the most recent call wins.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```ts\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * import { registerServerFunctionWrap } from "rwsdk/worker";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * import * as Sentry from "@sentry/cloudflare";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * registerServerFunctionWrap((fn, args, type) =>\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   Sentry.startSpan(\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     { name: fn.name, op: `function.rsc_${type}` },\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *     () => fn(...args)\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   )\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * );\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function registerServerFunctionWrap(wrap: ServerFunctionWrap): void;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @internal Reset the global wrap — only for tests.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function __resetServerFunctionWrap(): void;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Wrap a function to be used as a server query.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Method**: Defaults to `GET`. can be changed via `options`.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Behavior**: When called from the client, it returns data-only and does **not** rehydrate or re-render the React page.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Location**: Must be defined in a file with `"use server"`. We recommend `queries.ts` colocated with components.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Middleware**: You can pass an array of functions as the first argument to act as interruptors (e.g. for auth).\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```ts\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // getters.ts\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * "use server"\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * export const getUser = serverQuery(async (id: string) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return db.user.findUnique({ where: { id } })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function serverQuery<TArgs extends any[], TResult>(fnsOrFn: ServerFunction<TArgs, TResult> | [...Interruptor<TArgs>[], ServerFunction<TArgs, TResult>], options?: ServerFunctionOptions): WrappedServerFunction<TArgs, TResult>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * Wrap a function to be used as a server action.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Method**: Defaults to `POST`. can be changed via `options`.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Behavior**: When called from the client, it **will** rehydrate and re-render the React page with the new server state.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Location**: Must be defined in a file with `"use server"`. We recommend `actions.ts` colocated with components.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * - **Middleware**: You can pass an array of functions as the first argument to act as interruptors (e.g. for auth).\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * @example\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```ts\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * // actions.ts\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * "use server"\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * export const updateUser = serverAction(async (id: string, data: any) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' *   return db.user.update({ where: { id }, data })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * })\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * ```\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare function serverAction<TArgs extends any[], TResult>(fnsOrFn: ServerFunction<TArgs, TResult> | [...Interruptor<TArgs>[], ServerFunction<TArgs, TResult>], options?: ServerFunctionOptions): WrappedServerFunction<TArgs, TResult>;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export {};\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m''[39m,
                  isTruncated: [33mfalse[39m
                }
              },
              providerOptions: {
                vertex: {

                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'**Clarifying Contextual Data**\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m"I've realized my initial assumption was off; `getRequestInfo()` indeed doesn't directly expose `env`.                 [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m''[39m
            },
            {
              type: [32m'tool-call'[39m,

            {

                }

            }
          ]
        },
        {
          role: [32m'tool'[39m,
          content: [
            {
              type: [32m'tool-result'[39m,
              toolCallId: [32m'yTtLZ5VKK0VHwdFo'[39m,
              toolName: [32m'executeCommand'[39m,
              output: {
                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  output: [32m'import { jsx as _jsx, jsxs as _jsxs } from "react/jsx-runtime";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { normalizeActionResult } from "./render/normalizeActionResult";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { renderDocumentHtmlStream } from "./render/renderDocumentHtmlStream";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { renderToRscStream } from "./render/renderToRscStream";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { injectRSCPayload } from "rsc-html-stream/server";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { ErrorResponse } from "./error";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { createRscActionHandler } from "./register/worker";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { getRequestInfo, runWithRequestInfo, runWithRequestInfoOverrides, } from "./requestInfo/worker";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { ssrWebpackRequire } from "./imports/worker";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { defineRoutes } from "./lib/router";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { generateNonce } from "./lib/utils";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export * from "./requestInfo/types";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export const defineApp = (routes, options = {}) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    const router = defineRoutes(routes);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    const rscActionHandler = createRscActionHandler({\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        allowedOrigins: options.allowedOrigins,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        __rwRoutes: routes,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        fetch: async (request, env, cf) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            globalThis.__webpack_require__ = ssrWebpackRequire;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // context(justinvdm, 17 Mar 2026): Strip the Vite base path from the\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // request URL so that routes defined as "/" match requests to "/app/"\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m"            // when base: '/app/' is configured. Vite injects BASE_URL automatically.\r\n"[39m +
                    [32m'            const base = import.meta.env.BASE_URL;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if (base && base !== "/") {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const url = new URL(request.url);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                if (url.pathname.startsWith(base)) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    url.pathname = "/" + url.pathname.slice(base.length);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    request = new Request(url.toString(), request);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // context(justinvdm, 5 Feb 2025): Serve assets requests using the assets service binding\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // todo(justinvdm, 5 Feb 2025): Find a way to avoid this so asset requests are served directly\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // rather than first needing to go through the worker\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if (request.url.includes("/assets/")) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const url = new URL(request.url);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                url.pathname = url.pathname.slice("/assets/".length);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return env.ASSETS.fetch(new Request(url.toString(), request));\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            else if (import.meta.env.VITE_IS_DEV_SERVER &&\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                new URL(request.url).pathname === "/__worker-run") {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const expectedToken = import.meta.env\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    .VITE_RWSDK_WORKER_RUN_TOKEN;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const requestToken = request.headers.get("x-rwsdk-worker-run-token");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                if (!expectedToken || expectedToken !== requestToken) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    return new Response("Forbidden", { status: 403 });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const url = new URL(request.url);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const scriptPath = url.searchParams.get("script");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                if (!scriptPath) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m`                    return new Response("Missing 'script' query parameter", {\r\n`[39m +
                    [32m'                        status: 400,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                try {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const scriptModule = await import(/* @vite-ignore */ scriptPath);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (scriptModule.default) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        await scriptModule.default(request, env, cf);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    return new Response("Script executed successfully");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                catch (e) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    console.error(`Error executing script: ${scriptPath}\\n\\n${e.stack}`);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    return new Response(`Error executing script: ${e.message}`, {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        status: 500,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            else if (import.meta.env.VITE_IS_DEV_SERVER &&\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                request.url.includes("/__vite_preamble__")) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m`                return new Response('import RefreshRuntime from "/@react-refresh"; RefreshRuntime.injectIntoGlobalHook(window); window.$RefreshReg$ = () => {}; window.$RefreshSig$ = () => (type) => type; window.__vite_plugin_react_preamble_installed__ = true;', {\r\n`[39m +
                    [32m'                    headers: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        "content-type": "text/javascript",\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    },\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            try {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const url = new URL(request.url);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                let path = url.pathname;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                if (path !== "/" && !path.endsWith("/")) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    path = path + "/";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const isRSCRequest = url.searchParams.has("__rsc") ||\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    request.headers.get("accept")?.includes("text/x-component");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const isAction = url.searchParams.has("__rsc_action_id");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const rw = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    Document: DefaultDocument,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    nonce: generateNonce(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    rscPayload: true,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    ssr: true,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    databases: new Map(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    scriptsToBeLoaded: new Set(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    entryScripts: new Set(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    inlineScripts: new Set(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    pageRouteResolved: undefined,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const userResponseInit = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    status: 200,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    headers: new Headers(),\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const outerRequestInfo = {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    request,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    path,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    cf,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    params: {},\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    ctx: {},\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    rw,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    response: userResponseInit,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    isAction,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const createPageElement = (requestInfo, Page) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    let pageElement;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (isClientReference(Page)) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        const { ctx, params } = requestInfo; // context(justinvdm, 25 Feb 2025): If the page is a client reference, we need to avoid passing\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m"                        // down props the client shouldn't get (e.g. env). For safety, we pick the allowed props explicitly.\r\n"[39m +
                    [32m'                        pageElement = _jsx(Page, { ctx: ctx, params: params });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    else {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        pageElement = _jsx(Page, { ...requestInfo });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    return pageElement;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                const renderPage = async (requestInfo, Page, _onError) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (isClientReference(requestInfo.rw.Document)) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        if (import.meta.env.DEV) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            console.error("Document cannot be a client component");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        return new Response(null, {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            status: 500,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    // context(justinvdm, 2026-03-17): Capture rendering errors so the\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    // router can throw them and route to except handlers. We store the\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    // error on rw.renderError instead of throwing from renderPage, because\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m"                    // throwing mid-render corrupts React's internal RSC stream state\r\n"[39m +
                    [32m'                    // (causing "chunk.reason.enqueueModel is not a function" in subsequent\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    // renders). By returning normally, streams are cleaned up properly.\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const onError = (error) => {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        if (!rw.renderError) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            rw.renderError = error;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    };\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const actionResult = normalizeActionResult(requestInfo.rw.actionResult);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const isDataOnly = request.headers.get("x-rsc-data-only") === "true";\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const pageElement = isDataOnly && actionResult !== undefined\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        ? null\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        : createPageElement(requestInfo, Page);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (pageElement === null) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        rw.pageRouteResolved?.resolve();\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    const { rscPayload: shouldInjectRSCPayload } = rw;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    let rscPayloadStream = renderToRscStream({\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        input: {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            node: pageElement,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            actionResult,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        },\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        onError,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (isRSCRequest) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        const responseHeaders = new Headers(userResponseInit.headers);\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        responseHeaders.set("content-type", "text/x-component; charset=utf-8");\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        return new Response(rscPayloadStream, {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            status: userResponseInit.status,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            statusText: userResponseInit.statusText,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            headers: responseHeaders,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    let injectRSCPayloadStream;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    if (shouldInjectRSCPayload) {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        const [rscPayloadStream1, rscPayloadStream2] = rscPayloadStream.tee();\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        rscPayloadStream = rscPayloadStream1;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        injectRSCPayloadStream = injectRSCPayload(rscPayloadStream2, {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            nonce: rw.nonce,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    let html;\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    try {\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        html = await renderDocumentHtmlStream({\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            rscPayloadStream: rscPayloadStream,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            Document: rw.Document,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            requestInfo: requestInfo,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            onError,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                            shouldSSR: rw.ssr,\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                        });\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    }\r\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    catch (renderError) {\r\n'[39m +

              toolCallId: [32m'tv4bxlneINL5DQf6'[39m,
              toolName: [32m'applyDiff'[39m,
              input: {
                path: [32m'/home/user/myproject/src/app/api/test.ts'[39m,
                searchContent: [32m'import { route } from "rwsdk/router";\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'export const testRoutes = [\n'[39m +
                  [32m'  route("/api/sessions/test", (requestInfo) => {\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ \n'[39m +

                  [32m'];'[39m,
                replaceContent: [32m'import { route } from "rwsdk/router";\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'export const testRoutes = [\n'[39m +
                  [32m'  route("/api/sessions/test", () => {\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ \n'[39m +
                  [32m'      processEnvKeys: typeof process !== "undefined" ? Object.keys(process.env) : []\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    }), {\n'[39m +
                  [32m'      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    });\n'[39m +
                  [32m'  }),\n'[39m +
                  [32m'];'[39m,
                expectedReplacements: [33m1[39m
              },
              providerOptions: {
                vertex: {
                  thoughtSignature: [32m''[39m
                }
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          role: [32m'tool'[39m,
          content: [
            {
              type: [32m'tool-result'[39m,

      tools: [
        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'applyDiff'[39m,
          description: [32m"This is a tool for editing files. For moving or renaming files, you should generally use the executeCommand tool with the 'mv' command instead. For larger edits, use the writeFile tool to overwrite files.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m"Before using this tool, use the readFile tool to understand the file's contents and context\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'To make a file edit, provide the following:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. path: The path to the file to modify (relative to the current working directory, or an absolute path)\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. searchContent: The text to replace (must match the file contents exactly, including all whitespace and indentation)\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. replaceContent: The edited text to replace the searchContent (must be different from searchContent)\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. expectedReplacements: The number of replacements you expect to make. Defaults to 1 if not specified.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'By default, the tool will replace ONE occurrence of searchContent with replaceContent in the specified file. If you want to replace multiple occurrences, provide the expectedReplacements parameter with the exact number of occurrences you expect.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS FOR USING THIS TOOL:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. UNIQUENESS (when expectedReplacements is not specified): The searchContent MUST uniquely identify the specific instance you want to change. This means:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context BEFORE the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context AFTER the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include all whitespace, indentation, and surrounding code exactly as it appears in the file\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. EXPECTED MATCHES: If you want to replace multiple instances:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Use the expectedReplacements parameter with the exact number of occurrences you expect to replace\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - This will replace ALL occurrences of the searchContent with the replaceContent\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - If the actual number of matches doesn't equal expectedReplacements, the edit will fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'   - This is a safety feature to prevent unintended replacements\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. VERIFICATION: Before using this tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Check how many instances of the target text exist in the file\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If multiple instances exist, either:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     a) Gather enough context to uniquely identify each one and make separate calls, OR\n'[39m +
            [32m'     b) Use expectedReplacements parameter with the exact count of instances you expect to replace\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'WARNING:\n'[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if searchContent matches multiple locations and expectedReplacements isn't specified\n"[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if the number of matches doesn't equal expectedReplacements when it's specified\n"[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if searchContent doesn't match the file contents exactly (including whitespace)\n"[39m +
            [32m'- The tool will fail if searchContent and replaceContent are the same\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When making edits:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Ensure the edit results in idiomatic, correct code\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Do not add trailing whitespace to lines (a newline at the end of a file is fine)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Do not leave the code in a broken state\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'If you want to create a new file, use:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - A new file path, including dir name if needed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - An empty searchContent\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - The new file's contents as replaceContent\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Remember: when making multiple file edits in a row to the same file, you should prefer to send all edits in a single message with multiple calls to this tool, rather than multiple messages with a single call each.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'You may see the following fields in the result:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- autoFormattingEdits: If the auto-formatter makes any changes, this field will contain a diff against the file content after your edits and any user edits have been applied.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- newProblems: If any new problems are found after the edit, this field will contain information about them.'[39m,
          inputSchema: {
            [32m'$schema'[39m: [32m'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'[39m,
            type: [32m'object'[39m,
            properties: {
              path: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,
                description: [32m'The path of the file to modify (relative to the current working directory, or an absolute path).'[39m
              },
              searchContent: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,
                description: [32m'The text to replace.'[39m
              },
              replaceContent: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,

                    multiSelect: {
                      default: [33mfalse[39m,
                      description: [32m'Set to true to allow the user to select multiple options instead of just one. Use when choices are not mutually exclusive.'[39m,
                      type: [32m'boolean'[39m

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'attemptCompletion'[39m,
          description: [32m"Once you've received the results of tool uses and can confirm that the task is complete, use this tool to present the result of your work to the user.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'You MUST NOT generate any text before this tool call. All conclusion text must be included within the result parameter of the attemptCompletion tool.\n'[39m +
            [32m'Never use this tool with a question or request to engage in further conversation! Formulate the end of your result in a way that is final and does not require further input from the user.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'IMPORTANT: This tool CANNOT be used in combination with other tools (except todoWrite) in a single step. If you need to use other tools, you must do so in a separate step before calling this tool.'[39m,
          inputSchema: {
            [32m'$schema'[39m: [32m'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'[39m,
            type: [32m'object'[39m,
            properties: {
              result: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,
                description: [32m'The result of the task. Formulate this result in a way that is final and does not require further input from the user.'[39m
              }
            },
            required: [
              [32m'result'[39m
            ],
            additionalProperties: [33mfalse[39m
          }
        },
        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'executeCommand'[39m,
          description: [32m'Executes a given bash command in a persistent shell session with optional timeout, ensuring proper handling and security measures.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'IMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Before executing the command, please follow these steps:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Directory Verification:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use listFiles to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - For example, before running "mkdir foo/bar", first use listFiles to check that "foo" exists and is the intended parent directory\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Command Execution:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., cd "path with spaces/file.txt")\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Examples of proper quoting:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd "/Users/name/My Documents" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python "/path/with spaces/script.py" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Capture the output of the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Usage notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The command argument is required.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- You can specify an optional timeout in seconds (up to 300s / 5 minutes). If not specified, commands will timeout after 60s (1 minute).\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If the output exceeds 30000 characters, output will be truncated before being returned to you.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When issuing multiple commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple executeCommand tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run "git status" and "git diff", send a single message with two executeCommand tool calls in parallel.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single executeCommand call with \'&&\' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before executeCommand for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'  - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)\n'[39m +
            [32m"- You shall avoid use the markdown code block syntax (backtick, '`') in your command, as it will be interpreted as a command substitution.\n"[39m +
            [32m"- Before using this tool, you must first think about the context provided in <system-reminder> to understand the user's environment and tailor your commands to ensure they are compatible with their system. You must also consider if the command you need to run should be executed in a specific directory rather than the current working directory, and properly set the `cwd` if necessary.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Committing changes with git\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Only create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Git Safety Protocol:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m"- NEVER run destructive git commands (push --force, reset --hard, checkout ., restore ., clean -f, branch -D) unless the user explicitly requests these actions. Taking unauthorized destructive actions is unhelpful and can result in lost work, so it's best to ONLY run these commands when given direct instructions \n"[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- CRITICAL: Always create NEW commits rather than amending, unless the user explicitly requests a git amend. When a pre-commit hook fails, the commit did NOT happen  — so --amend would modify the PREVIOUS commit, which may result in destroying work or losing previous changes. Instead, after hook failure, fix the issue, re-stage, and create a NEW commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When staging files, prefer adding specific files by name rather than using "git add -A" or "git add .", which can accidentally include sensitive files (.env, credentials) or large binaries\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the executeCommand tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files. IMPORTANT: Never use the -uall flag as it can cause memory issues on large repos.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Run a git log command to see recent commit messages, so that you can follow this repository's commit message style.\n"[39m +
            [32m'2. Analyze all staged changes (both previously staged and newly added) and draft a commit message:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Summarize the nature of the changes (eg. new feature, enhancement to an existing feature, bug fix, refactoring, test, docs, etc.). Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. "add" means a wholly new feature, "update" means an enhancement to an existing feature, "fix" means a bug fix, etc.).\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Draft a concise (1-2 sentences) commit message that focuses on the "why" rather than the "what"\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Ensure it accurately reflects the changes and their purpose\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Add relevant untracked files to the staging area.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create the commit with a message ending with:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Co-Authored-By: Pochi <noreply@getpochi.com>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: Replace <Share URL> with the Share URL from system information if available. If not available, omit the " | [Task](...)" part.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run git status after the commit completes to verify success.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: git status depends on the commit completing, so run it sequentially after the commit.\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook changes, retry the commit ONCE to include these automated changes. If it fails again, it usually means a pre-commit hook is preventing the commit. If the commit succeeds but you notice that files were modified by the pre-commit hook, you MUST amend your commit to include them.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run additional commands to read or explore code, besides git bash commands\n'[39m +
            [32m'- DO NOT push to the remote repository unless the user explicitly asks you to do so\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Never use git commands with the -i flag (like git rebase -i or git add -i) since they require interactive input which is not supported.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Do not use --no-edit with git rebase commands, as the --no-edit flag is not a valid option for git rebase.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- In order to ensure good formatting, ALWAYS pass the commit message via a HEREDOC, a la this example:\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m`git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'\n`[39m +
            [32m'Commit message here.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Co-Authored-By: Pochi <noreply@getpochi.com>\n'[39m +
            [32m'EOF\n'[39m +
            [32m')"\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Creating pull requests\n'[39m +
            [32m'Use the gh command via the executeCommand tool for ALL GitHub-related tasks including working with issues, pull requests, checks, and releases. If given a Github URL use the gh command to get the information needed.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'IMPORTANT: When the user asks you to create a pull request, follow these steps carefully:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel using the executeCommand tool, in order to understand the current state of the branch since it diverged from the main branch:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git status command to see all untracked files (never use -uall flag)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Check if the current branch tracks a remote branch and is up to date with the remote, so you know if you need to push to the remote\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git log command and `git diff [base-branch]...HEAD` to understand the full commit history for the current branch (from the time it diverged from the base branch)\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Analyze all changes that will be included in the pull request, making sure to look at all relevant commits (NOT just the latest commit, but ALL commits that will be included in the pull request!!!), and draft a pull request title and summary:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Keep the PR title short (under 70 characters)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Use the description/body for details, not the title\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands in parallel:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create new branch if needed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Push to remote with -u flag if needed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create PR using gh pr create with the format below. Use a HEREDOC to pass the body to ensure correct formatting.\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m`gh pr create --title "the pr title" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'\n`[39m +
            [32m'## Summary\n'[39m +
            [32m'<1-3 bullet points>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## Test plan\n'[39m +
            [32m'[Checklist of TODOs for testing the pull request...]\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'EOF\n'[39m +
            [32m')"\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git confi'[39m... 355 more characters,
          inputSchema: {
            [32m'$schema'[39m: [32m'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'[39m,

              command: {

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'useSkill'[39m,
          description: [32m'Execute a skill within the main conversation\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask about a SPECIFIC skill (e.g., "How does the pdf skill work?", "What does the commit skill do?"), you must call this tool to get detailed information about that skill.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask general questions like "What skills are available?" or "List all skills", simply refer to the "Available skills" section below - do NOT call this tool.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m"This tool returns the skill's detailed instructions which you must then follow to complete the task. The instructions contain step-by-step guidance on how to perform the specific task or workflow.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'How to invoke:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Use this tool with the skill name\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Example: `skill: "pdf-processing"` - invoke the pdf-processing skill\n'[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will return the skill's instructions\n"[39m +
            [32m'- Follow the returned instructions carefully to complete the task\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When a skill is relevant for a task OR when users ask about a SPECIFIC skill, you must invoke this tool IMMEDIATELY as your first action\n'[39m +
            [32m'- For general questions about available skills, simply refer to the "Available skills" list below without calling this tool\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER just announce or mention a skill in your text response without actually calling this tool (except for general skill listing requests)\n'[39m +
            [32m'- This is a BLOCKING REQUIREMENT: invoke the relevant Skill tool BEFORE generating any other response about a specific skill or task\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Only use skills listed in "Available skills" below\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Check compatibility requirements before using a skill - ensure the skill is compatible with the current OS/environment\n'[39m +
            [32m'- After calling this tool, follow the returned instructions step by step\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The skill file location is shown in the [Location: filepath] section of each skill listing below - use this information to understand where the skill is defined\n'[39m +
            [32m"- Use the directory containing the skill's source file as the base directory for resolving any resource files mentioned in the instructions\n"[39m +
            [32m'- Proactively explore the skill directory for optional resources that enhance task completion:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * scripts/ - executable code that agents can run (Python, Bash, JavaScript, etc.)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * references/ - on-demand documentation (REFERENCE.md, FORMS.md, domain-specific files)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * assets/ - static resources (templates, images, data files, schemas)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  Use these resources when they exist and are relevant to the current task\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Available skills:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'- **find-skills**: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. [Location: _builtIn_]\n'[39m +
            [32m'- **create-skill**: Helps users create new custom agent skills. Use when the user wants to create a new skill, automate a workflow, or package specialized knowledge for reuse. [Location: _builtIn_]'[39m,
          inputSchema: {
            [32m'$schema'[39m: [32m'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'[39m,

            properties: {
              skill: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,

              }
